name: Thrysos, King of Revels
description:
Thrysos, King of Revels

*{Lore Where torches flare and cups spill, where dancers stamp until dawn, there strides Thrysos crowned with ivy and horn. He is both host and beast, a patron of license who laughs at law. In his train follow the masked and the wild, the broken who dance to forget, the bold who revel to remember, and the lustful who seek the heat of bodies in the dark. His rites intoxicate, inflame, and unmask --- yet each cup leaves a mark, each kiss a chain, each night of abandon a dawn of reckoning.

Thrysos rules the spectrum from the grinning drunkard who sings bawdy songs in taverns to the ecstatic cults whose rites strip away not only clothes but every pretense of civilization. He is the god of the Bacchanalia, the wild hunt of the senses, the moment when the watcher becomes the participant and the participant becomes the sacrifice. In his milder aspect, he blesses the fertility of the harvest and the laughter of lovers. In his darker aspect, he drives maenads to tear apart the hero who refused to join the dance.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Thrysos is called by many names. In Linn, his followers are the Storm-Dancers, who whirl through gales until exhaustion, their cries mistaken for ecstasy. In Aelinnel, he is the Unbounded Guest, whose arrival at a feast is marked by laughter, shattered glass, and at least one scandalous elopement. In Zakov, the Revel-King is invoked before every pirate carouse, with the understanding that a hungover crew is a vulnerable one --- and that the captain's bed is never empty. In Vhasian hedge-courts, his name is whispered by those who seek a potion to lower inhibitions or a curse of unquenchable desire. His rituals are never secret --- they are too loud, too wet, too fragrant with spilled wine and sweat --- but they are always dangerous, for Thrysos demands that every excess be paid for, if not tonight then at the first hangover of spring, or in the unwanted child, or in the blood feud started by a jealous spouse.

Among the signs of his presence are wine that never sours, a mask that smiles without a wearer, goat-tracks circling thrice before vanishing, the sudden warmth of a stranger's hand on your arm, and the scent of myrrh and sweat clinging to a garment never worn.

Thrysos promises freedom without end: no oaths, no chains, only song and cup and flesh. Yet those who drink too deeply wake to find they serve not joy but appetite --- and appetite is a master that never sleeps.

quote "Drink, and be unafraid. Kiss, and be unbound. Dance, and be devoured. Every mask is true when worn long enough, and every truth is a mask for hunger." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Ecstatic Release: dance, intoxication, the shedding of inhibition (and clothing) Masked Freedom: identity as performance, truth through disguise, the thrill of the anonymous encounter Feast and Famine: excess, appetite, lust, and the cost of indulgence Ritual Frenzy: communal ecstasy that transforms participants into something feral and beautiful The Bacchic Dark: the point where pleasure tips into violence, where the maenad tears apart the intruder itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Thrysos does not bind a familiar of quiet habits. Their Thiasos is a small beast or being of primal appetites: a satyr-kid with too-bright eyes that nibbles at wine-soaked bread, a fox that dances on its hind legs when the moon is full, a ferret that steals silk ribbons from lovers' bedposts, a gecko that clings to wine jugs and changes color with desire, or a small bronze cymbal that claps of its own accord during moments of flirtation. This creature requires no ordinary food, but it must be present during an act of indulgence (drinking, dancing, lovemaking, or feasting) each day. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it shrieks at inappropriate moments, or its antics become grotesque, imposing --1 die on all social rolls involving subtlety or discretion for its bearer).

The Codex of Thrysos is never a sober book. It may be a wine-stained journal of erotic poetry, a set of ivory tablets carved with scenes of revelry, a leather folio containing pressed flowers from orgies, or a scroll that unravels further each time the reader laughs. Because Thrysos's truths are written in the body's language, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week adding a new verse, sketch, or memory of indulgence, or the Rites stored there become dull (treat as Compromised until reinvigorated with a night of celebration).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Thrysos may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a word of desire. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Performance or Sway rolls involving seduction, charm, or intimidation, and the first time each scene the bearer would be affected by a Fear effect, they may laugh it off (ignore it). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Thrysos.
summary: Thrysos, King of Revels *{Lore Where torches flare and cups spill, where dancers stamp until dawn, there strides Thrysos crowned with ivy and horn. He is both host and beast, a patron of license who laughs at law.

rites.name: Rite of the Overflowing Cup
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A brimming vessel of wine (or a more intimate offering). Effect: Allies who drink gain +1 die to social rolls this scene; enemies who drink suffer --1 die. Those who drink deeply may find their inhibitions loosened in other ways (GM's discretion for romantic or scandalous complications). Push It: All in Near must test Resolve (DV 3) or be swept into revelry, possibly leading to unplanned liaisons or public confessions; mark 1 SB (Hearts). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Allies who drink gain +1 die to social rolls this scene; enemies who drink suffer --1 die. Those who drink deeply may find their inhibitions loosened in other ways (GM's discretion for romantic or scandalous complications). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Horned Dance
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: A mask or antlers (carved from a stag that died in rut). Effect: Enter an ecstatic frenzy: +2 dice to melee or performance; ignore 1 Fatigue per round. Your movements become both seductive and threatening; those watching may be captivated or disturbed. Push It: The frenzy spreads: one ally joins you in the dance, but both mark 1 SB (Spades) as the lines between combat and carnality blur. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Enter an ecstatic frenzy: +2 dice to melee or performance; ignore 1 Fatigue per round. Your movements become both seductive and threatening; those watching may be captivated or disturbed. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Breaking Mask
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Instant; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A mask smashed in hand (or a lover's token torn in two). Effect: Reveal the true face beneath the social mask: the target suffers --2 dice to disguise, deceit, or composure until scene end. They may also be compelled to admit a hidden desire (GM's discretion). Push It: Truth is forced: the target must declare one hidden desire aloud, possibly to the wrong person; mark 1 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: Reveal the true face beneath the social mask: the target suffers --2 dice to disguise, deceit, or composure until scene end. They may also be compelled to admit a hidden desire (GM's discretion). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Wild Procession
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , Materials: A horn sounded thrice (or a kiss passed around a circle). Effect: Summon spectral revelers --- satyrs, dancers, horned riders, and a few figures whose embraces are more than friendly. For the scene, the zone is ed: hostile entry forces 1 SB (Spades); allies gain +1 die to resist compulsion or fear. The revelers may also grant a boon of Vigor to one couple (restore 1 Fatigue to two allies who share a bond of intimacy). Push It: The procession spills beyond the zone; outsiders in Near must test Resolve (DV 4) or join the dance --- and perhaps the revel's other activities. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Summon spectral revelers --- satyrs, dancers, horned riders, and a few figures whose embraces are more than friendly. For the scene, the zone is ed: hostile entry forces 1 SB (Spades); allies gain +1 die to resist compulsion or fear. The revelers may also grant a boon of Vigor to one couple (restore 1 Fatigue to two allies who share a bond of intimacy). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Endless Feast
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A feast laid for twelve, with wine, music, and comfortable cushions. Effect: For one scene, the zone floods with plenty: allies restore Fatigue; all rolls tied to appetite or indulgence gain +2 dice. Leaving requires Resolve (DV 3) --- the comforts are hard to abandon. The feast also grants a temporary Heightened Desire condition: the first seduction or intimidation roll each ally makes gains +1 die. Push It: The feast persists as a hunger-haunt; start Hedonist's Curse [6]. Participants may find themselves unable to derive pleasure from mundane means, always craving the excess of that night. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For one scene, the zone floods with plenty: allies restore Fatigue; all rolls tied to appetite or indulgence gain +2 dice. Leaving requires Resolve (DV 3) --- the comforts are hard to abandon. The feast also grants a temporary Heightened Desire condition: the first seduction or intimidation roll each ally makes gains +1 die. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Reveler's Apotheosis
rites.description: (Master, 15 XP) Extended; Touch; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A crown of twisted vines that bleed when touched, the first laugh of a newborn, a promise broken at a crossroads, and the willing touch of a lover. Effect: Grant the ultimate reveler's gift --- transformation into pure ecstatic essence. The target becomes immune to fear and despair, gains +3 dice to performance or frenzy, and can inspire allies within Near (+1 die to all rolls). Their aura also carries a scent of desire; anyone within Near who sees them must test Resolve (DV 3) or be mildly infatuated (disadvantage on hostile actions). Push It: The transformation becomes contagious --- nearby allies may also transform but risk losing their humanity (their sense of self blurs into the ecstatic mob); mark +2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +4) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Permanent.
rites.effect: Grant the ultimate reveler's gift --- transformation into pure ecstatic essence. The target becomes immune to fear and despair, gains +3 dice to performance or frenzy, and can inspire allies within Near (+1 die to all rolls). Their aura also carries a scent of desire; anyone within Near who sees them must test Resolve (DV 3) or be mildly infatuated (disadvantage on hostile actions). \\
